text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each merge triggers an automated build and runs a suite of tests in a CI pipeline. This process is crucial because it catches issues early, before they have a chance to grow into larger problems. By running tests automatically, developers get immediate feedback on whether their code is working correctly. As a result, teams can maintain a stable codebase, reduce manual errors, and speed up the overall development cycle.
